chart.title: Share of the population with tertiary education
chart.subtitle: Percentage of the population aged 25 to 65 years who have either completed or partially completed [tertiary](#dod:tertiary-education) education.
chart.note: Data for the years before 2015 are estimates, while data from 2015 onwards are projections.
chart.citation: Barro and Lee (2015); Lee and Lee (2016)

Combined - percentage of 25-64 years adu.titleShort: Share of 25-64 year olds with incomplete tertiary education
Combined - percentage of 25-64 years adu.titleLong: Share of 25-64 year olds with incomplete tertiary education
Combined - percentage of 25-64 years adu.descriptionProcessing:
Historical data up to the year 2010 has been sourced from 'Human Capital in the Long Run' by Lee and Lee (2016). This historical data was then combined with recent projections provided by Barro ane Lee (2015).

Regional aggregates were computed by Our World in Data through yearly population-weighted averages, where annual values are proportionally adjusted to emphasize the influence of larger populations.
Combined - percentage of 25-64 years adu.citationShort: Barro and Lee (2015); Lee and Lee (2016) – with major processing by Our World in Data
Combined - percentage of 25-64 years adu.citationLong: Barro and Lee (2015); Lee and Lee (2016) – with major processing by Our World in Data. “Share of 25-64 year olds with incomplete tertiary education” [dataset]. Barro and Lee, “Projections of Educational Attainment”; Lee and Lee, “Human Capital in the Long Run” [original data].
